title: Citrus × limonia otaitensis
intro: Citrus × limonia otaitensis, also known as Tahiti lime or Tahitian lime, is a naturalized species of citrus tree native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its small, yellowish-green fruit and aromatic flowers. The health benefits of Citrus × limonia otaitensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include limonene, beta-pinene, and limonin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Citrus × limonia otaitensis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, juice, and extract, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Citrus × limonia otaitensis is classified as a species within the genus Citrus of the family Rutaceae. It is a hybrid of the rough lemon (Citrus × jambhiri) and the sudachi (Citrus sudachi). This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup. The species is native to the Ryukyu Islands, specifically the island of Okinawa, in Japan. As a member of the Rutaceae family, Citrus × limonia otaitensis is part of the larger citrus family that includes other citrus species such as oranges and lemons.
properties: Citrus × limonia otaitensis, also known as the Tahiti lime or Rangpur lime, is believed to possess various therapeutic properties due to its high vitamin C content and bioactive compounds. Studies have suggested that extracts from Citrus × limonia otaitensis may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's essential oils have been found to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, which could potentially contribute to its therapeutic properties. Additionally, Citrus × limonia otaitensis has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the therapeutic potential of Citrus × limonia otaitensis.
parts: Citrus × limonia otaitensis, also known as the Tahiti lime, is a species of citrus tree. The leaves of Citrus × limonia otaitensis contain flavonoids and limonoids, which are believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Citrus × limonia otaitensis have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
